title: Business Registration (Sole Proprietorship)
description: Register a sole proprietorship business name at your Divisional Secretariat within 14 days of starting trade.
department: Divisional Secretariat / Registrar of Business Names

offices.name: Your Divisional Secretariat
offices.address: Nearest DS office to your business address

documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC) — original + copy
Completed Business Registration Form (from DS office)
Proof of business address (utility bill or rental agreement)
Two passport-size photographs
steps: Choose your business name and check it is not already registered
Collect the registration form from your Divisional Secretariat
Submit the form with NIC and address proof within 14 days of commencing business
Pay the registration fee at the DS cashier
Collect your Business Registration Certificate
tips: You must register within 14 days of starting business — late registration can incur fines
Keep copies of the certificate — banks require it to open a business account
common_mistakes: Starting trade before registering the business name
Using a name too similar to an existing registered business

title: Land / Property Title Registration
description: Register or transfer ownership of land and property through the Land Registry.
documents_required: Original Title Deed (or prior registration documents)
National Identity Card of all parties
Survey plan approved by Survey Department (if new subdivision)
Stamp duty payment receipt
Notarized transfer deed (for sales/gifts)
steps: Engage a notary to prepare the transfer deed
Pay stamp duty at a designated bank
Submit documents at the relevant Land Registry office
Registry verifies title chain and survey details
Collect registered title upon approval

fees.registration: Varies by property value (stamp duty + registration fees)

tips: Always verify the title chain with a lawyer before purchasing property
Survey plan must match physical boundaries — disputes are common
common_mistakes: Missing stamp duty payment before submission
Title deed names not matching NIC spelling exactly

title: Income Tax Filing / TIN Registration
description: Obtain a Tax Identification Number (TIN) and file annual income tax returns with the Inland Revenue Department.

offices.name: IRD Head Office
offices.address: Sir Chittampalam A. Gardiner Mawatha, Colombo 02

documents_required: National Identity Card (NIC)
Proof of address
Employment letter or business registration (if self-employed)
Bank statements and income records for tax filing
WHT certificates (if applicable)
steps: Register for a TIN online at irdezfile.ird.gov.lk or at an IRD office
Maintain records of all income and allowable deductions
File annual return before the IRD deadline (usually 30 November)
Pay any tax due via bank or online
Keep acknowledgement receipt for 6 years

fees.late_filing_penalty: Varies — penalties apply for late returns

processing_time: TIN: same day online; Return processing: weeks to months if audited
tips: File before the deadline even if you cannot pay — reduces penalties
Keep all WHT certificates from employers and banks
common_mistakes: Not registering for TIN when starting self-employment
Declaring incorrect income — triggers audit and penalties

title: Aswesuma Welfare Benefits
description: Apply for social welfare cash transfers under the Aswesuma programme for eligible low-income households.
department: Department of Samurdhi / Welfare Benefits Board
documents_required: National Identity Card of household head
Birth certificates of all household members
Proof of income (or lack thereof)
Grama Niladhari verification of household composition
Bank account details for benefit transfer
steps: Check eligibility criteria on the official welfare portal or at DS office
Collect application form during registration window
GN officer verifies household details (may include home visit)
Submit completed form to Divisional Secretariat
Await SMS/letter notification of approval and payment schedule
tips: Ensure all family members are listed — missing members can delay approval
Update the GN if your household composition changes
common_mistakes: Incomplete household member list on application
Not updating bank account details after approval

title: Pvt Ltd Company Registration (eROC)
description: Incorporate a private limited company online through the eROC portal.

offices.name: Registrar of Companies
offices.address: Level 12, West Tower, World Trade Center, Colombo 01

documents_required: Proposed company name (3 options)
Articles of Association
Form 1 (Company registration)
NIC/passport copies of all directors and shareholders
Registered office address proof
Consent letters from directors (Form 18)
steps: Reserve company name on eroc.drc.gov.lk
Prepare Articles of Association (often via company secretary)
Submit incorporation documents online with fees
ROC reviews and issues Certificate of Incorporation
Register for TIN and EPF within statutory deadlines

fees.incorporation: LKR 15,000–30,000+ (depends on share capital and services)

tips: Engage a certified company secretary — required for Pvt Ltd incorporation
Register for TIN and EPF immediately after incorporation
common_mistakes: Name reservation expiring before full submission

title: Traffic Fine Payment (GovPay)
description: Pay police traffic spot fines instantly via mobile banking through the GovPay platform.
documents_required: Spot fine receipt issued by officer (with reference number)
NIC or driving license (for verification if paying in person)
steps: Receive spot fine slip from traffic officer with payment reference
Open your bank or fintech app with GovPay integration
Enter fine reference number and confirm amount
Complete payment — officer receives confirmation electronically
Keep digital receipt

fees.fine_amount: As stated on spot fine slip

tips: Pay promptly — unresolved fines can affect license renewal
GovPay works with most major Sri Lankan banks and fintech apps
common_mistakes: Entering wrong reference number in banking app
Losing the paper slip before payment
